title: Tokyo 3 population issues
wikitext:
[]: Tokyo-3 population issues             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Sun Jan 04, 2009 2:14 am (Split from this thread ) There isn't enough information to explain it other than another plothole. I mean, are all of the reconstructed buildings repopulated as well? Is there a long line to get into Tokyo-3, regardless of the average mortality rate there?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Jan 04, 2009 5:59 am That post merits a separate thread of its own- there's quite a bit of room for discussion there, although "LOL plothole" will inevitably turn up again and again. The " ghetto " as Dr. Nick put it is where most of the population likely lives and works.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sun Jan 04, 2009 9:19 am I don't think Tokyo-3 has a mojor poulation issue. I don't think it has an issue at all. As the series progresses with all the angel attacks back and fourth people start to move out by the truck load.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Sun Jan 04, 2009 3:09 pm With the near apocalypse that was 2nd Impact it's reasonable to assume many cities were totaled. So there may well be a queue of people trying to get into Tokyo III. And what with all the man power NERV needs there probably loads of jobs.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Sun Jan 04, 2009 6:34 pm There weren't high mortality rates. Gaghiel and Sandalphon didn't damage Tokyo-3 Matariel probably didn't either. Explosions from Israfel and Sahaquiel might have rocked the shelters. Not too many died in the Sachiel attack. Arael and Leliel didn't cause any explosions, although the suddenness of the attacks may have had people in the damaged buildings. The most damage Bardiel caused was an explosion outside of the city. Shamshel wasn't a surprise, probably only killed people if the shelter was rocked by 01's landing. Ramiel was probably the same way, hell, they didn't even EVACUATE. Iruel didn't kill anyone, and Zeruel, even when he sent 02's head into a shelter, didn't seem to damage them too much. Armisael had the entire city permanently evacuated, so the last two Angels are moot points (not that Kaworu would have killed anyone).

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Mon Jan 05, 2009 8:52 am Actually if Kaworu succeeded technically he would've killed EVERYONE . (Maybe... no one really knows) IN B4 "Nobody knew Kaworu's intention."

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Tue Jan 06, 2009 12:25 am judging by the fact that Shinji was supposed to live by himself in what i understood as an empty building, and the constant outpouring of citizens, not too many people lived there. Were there any other schools in the city? cuz fitting a cities population of kids in one school would have to mean low population as well.
